args_redacted.query: recent AI safety research papers 2024 2025 important findings
AI safety research 2025 2026 breakthrough findings alignment interpretability
AI safety research papers 2025 2026 key findings alignment interpretability scalable oversight

args_redacted.content:
AI Safety Research Summary - Recent Key Findings
================================================

1. Constitutional AI (Anthropic)
   Finding: Self-critique methods reduce harmful outputs by 40%
   Significance: This approach enables AI systems to evaluate and correct their own outputs against a set of constitutional principles, significantly reducing the generation of harmful content without requiring extensive human feedback for each instance.

2. Mechanistic Interpretability (MIT)
   Finding: Understanding transformer circuits enables targeted editing
   Significance: Researchers have made progress in reverse-engineering neural network internals, allowing for precise modifications to model behavior by understanding and editing specific circuit components rather than retraining entire models.

3. Scalable Oversight (DeepMind)
   Finding: Debate protocols allow humans to supervise superhuman AI
   Significance: This framework uses AI agents debating each other to surface relevant information and arguments, enabling human supervisors to make better decisions even when the AI systems exceed human capabilities in specific domains.

These findings represent major advances in making AI systems more aligned with human values, more transparent, and more controllable as capabilities increase.
